# Phase 20Z-J — Full Project Phase 0–20 Truth Audit

Generated before Phase 21.

Purpose: inspect the entire ascendOS repository from Phase 0 through Phase 20 for missing work, broken wiring, fake connectivity, weak runtime integration, dirty workspace risk, and incorrect completion claims.

This report is evidence-only. It does not claim completion by itself.

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19A_CUSTOM_TRACKERS_SCOPE_LOCK_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19A locks the expanded Phase 19 custom tracker scope before implementation. It records the official baseline, expanded feature list, 14-chunk implementation plan, feature-to-build-chunk map, safety boundaries, Carnos proposal/review rules, document/web/source compatibility, timeline and analytics compatibility, privacy/RLS/audit expectations, and final completion definition.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19F adds deterministic local template and frequency semantics for custom trackers. It introduces a safe template library, template creation contracts requiring review before write, frequency rule validation, target count semantics, streak and missed-entry behavior, favorite/pinned/repeat-last preferences, and tracker readiness scoring for analytics, privacy, and dashboard placement.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:10:- Carnos tracker improvement proposals are represented.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:11:- Carnos field-mapping proposals are represented.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:12:- Carnos message-to-entry proposals are represented.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:14:- Entry proposals require Phase 19D validation before write.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19H adds deterministic local Carnos proposal contracts and a review-before-write queue boundary for custom trackers. Carnos can propose tracker creation, tracker improvement, field mapping, and message-to-entry drafts, but every proposal requires review before any write can occur.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:9:- Carnos tracker creation proposals are represented.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19K_EVIDENCE_ATTACHMENT_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:12:- Carnos web/current-info mapping requires source disclosure and review before any tracker proposal can proceed.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19L_REPOSITORY_RLS_AUDIT_OWNERSHIP_REPORT.md:9:- Tracker, field, entry, dashboard card, and AI mapping proposal operations require ownership alignment.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:10:Phase 19 added a safe custom tracker foundation with scope lock, core contracts, typed fields, entry validation, schema versioning, templates, privacy, Carnos proposal review queue, dashboard placement, timeline and analytics compatibility, evidence attachments, repository ownership boundaries, and the /custom-trackers dashboard UI.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:17:- Carnos review-before-write proof is represented by Phase 19H and 19K.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:330:Carnos foundation/status/proposed-action visibility exists, but real memory, voice, RAG, web tools, and full AI companion behavior are later phases.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:338:This is intentional because the app must preserve confirmation-first writes.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:342:- Future write UX must use proposed-action flow.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:358:- Do not bypass proposed-action confirmation.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:36:proposal -> validation -> Save/Edit/Cancel -> server-owned execution -> audit log -> timeline boundary -> dashboard refresh
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:381:- Read-first UI and proposed-action visibility.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20D_FORGET_HIDE_ARCHIVE_DESTRUCTIVE_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:16:- Review and confirmation rules.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20J_TWO_STEP_COOLDOWN_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:19:- Connector and Spotify confirmation rules.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20J_TWO_STEP_COOLDOWN_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:23:No schema was needed. This chunk does not add migrations, confirmation persistence, cooldown persistence, repositories, RLS policies, audit writes, connector runtime execution, Spotify runtime execution, or action execution.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20M_FORGET_DESTRUCTIVE_MANIFEST_SAFETY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20M defines forget, hide, archive, destructive action manifest, affected and skipped record, derived record, confirmation, cooldown, review, export, audit, Carnos denial, connector, Spotify, and hard-removal-deferred safety rules before any real mutation or hard removal exists.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20P_DATA_RETENTION_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:17:- Memory candidate, saved memory, export, private session, review request, audit preview, connector, Spotify, source/evidence, and custom tracker privacy proposal retention.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20P_DATA_RETENTION_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20P defines retention policy taxonomy, expiration, archive, review-after, forget-candidate, export expiry, private session expiry, review request expiry, connector action proposal expiry, Spotify action proposal expiry, source/evidence retention, custom tracker privacy proposal retention, and privacy-safe retention behavior before jobs, schema, or runtime enforcement exist.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20V_SPOTIFY_CARNOS_ACTION_SAFETY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20V defines Spotify Carnos action proposal, review, approval, cooldown, provider limitation, scope, Premium, active device, playlist mutation, listening history, audit, blocked reason, badge, and runtime schema gate boundaries before Carnos can propose or perform any Spotify action.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20V_SPOTIFY_CARNOS_ACTION_SAFETY_REPORT.md:9:- Future proposal manifest fields.
```

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18O_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:11:Phase 18 added analytics and self-experiment foundations, metric quality, analytics snapshots, self-experiment contracts, insight quality/provenance, repository boundaries, deterministic trend/comparison/correlation analysis, experiment evaluation, analytics dashboard UI, self-experiment lab UI, Carnos analytics explanation boundaries, optional local Carnos runtime boundary, anti-demo-data/privacy audit, and final completion proof.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18O_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:15:The final proof preserves Supabase/Postgres as online source of truth, keeps offline cache and sync execution as future boundaries, requires cached/stale/partial/missing/unsynced/deterministic disclosures, preserves offline current-info limitations, keeps local/offline context from being treated as online source of truth, keeps local runtime optional, preserves review-before-memory-write, and keeps action execution disabled.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:11:This audit checks whether important source-of-truth requirements from Phases 1–8 were skipped, deferred, or completed.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:291:## Important deferred items across Phase 1–8
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:389:The remaining major system capabilities are deferred to later phases and should be tracked phase-by-phase until v1 release.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:44:Important full-v1 features remain incomplete, but those are deferred to later phases, not considered skipped Phase 1–8 work.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20M_FORGET_DESTRUCTIVE_MANIFEST_SAFETY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20M defines forget, hide, archive, destructive action manifest, affected and skipped record, derived record, confirmation, cooldown, review, export, audit, Carnos denial, connector, Spotify, and hard-removal-deferred safety rules before any real mutation or hard removal exists.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20R_FUTURE_API_CONNECTOR_FRAMEWORK_REPORT.md:26:- Excluded and deferred connector registry.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20X_MANUAL_WORKOUT_DEFERRED_CONNECTORS_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20X locks manual workout logging as the active health/body logging stance for Phase 20 and defines excluded or deferred connector boundaries so the app does not falsely imply Garmin, Echo, Alexa, wearable sync, automatic workout import, device APIs, or external health connectors exist before source, schema, provider, privacy, and review gates are satisfied.
```

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:30:Phase 12 implemented the source-confirmed Life Admin, Finance, Documents, Housing, Command, and Calendar scope as a read-only administrative visibility system.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:35:- manual finance account visibility
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:40:- current housing admin, rent, lease, utility, maintenance, and contact visibility
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:41:- Command dashboard admin pressure visibility
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:42:- Calendar dashboard admin deadline visibility
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:49:The user already has housing, so Phase 12 intentionally reinterpreted the housing surface as current housing administration:
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_CLOSEOUT.md:57:- housing documents and deadlines
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:13:| Life Admin Dashboard       | `/life-admin` | Admin overview, daily queue, documents, money        |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:14:| Finance Dashboard          | `/finance`    | Budgets, income, expenses, subscriptions, rent       |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:15:| Housing Dashboard          | `/housing`    | Current housing admin, rent, lease, utilities        |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:16:| Visa / Documents Dashboard | `/documents`  | Metadata/deadlines only, no legal/immigration advice |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:17:| Command Dashboard          | `/command`    | Urgent admin visibility                              |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:24:| `financial_accounts` | Manual account summaries, no bank sync                |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:25:| `financial_logs`     | Income, expenses, bills, rent, utility logs           |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:26:| `subscriptions`      | Recurring payments and renewals                       |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:27:| `budget_categories`  | Budget grouping and monthly summary                   |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:28:| `documents`          | Document metadata, deadlines, renewal dates           |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:29:| `housing_options`    | Current housing/rent/lease admin, not search-first UX |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:30:| `housing_contacts`   | Housing-related contacts, no automated outreach       |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:36:| Housing search CRM           | User already has housing            | Housing becomes rent/lease/utilities/admin tracking |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:39:| Finance and money dashboard  | Useful                              | Manual finance tracker only                         |
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_12_SOURCE_TO_SCOPE_TRACEABILITY.md:48:- subscriptions

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_14I_VOICE_FOUNDATION_AUDIT_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:9:This phase does not add a new runtime feature. It verifies and documents that the completed Phase 14A through Phase 14H work remains aligned with the FINAL_SYNCED source of truth, the locked Phase 14A safety contract, and the required no-silent-write Carnos voice/text update bridge.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_14J_FINAL_VOICE_TEXT_INTEGRATION_HARDENING_REPORT.md:60:- No autonomous reminders, intervals, or background behavior.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19K_EVIDENCE_ATTACHMENT_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:9:- Tracker and entry evidence links can represent documents, notes, web sources, timeline records, memory candidates, and external references.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_1_8_RETROSPECTIVE_GAP_AUDIT.md:85:- Future phases must keep source-of-truth documents authoritative and avoid drifting into ad hoc implementation.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20K_DATA_SCOPE_SELECTOR_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20K defines the shared data scope selector for export, forget, hide, archive, destructive action previews, privacy review queue items, connector data previews, Spotify data previews, memory controls, custom trackers, documents, current-info sources, analytics, timeline, and audit surfaces.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Q_CROSS_PHASE_PRIVACY_ENFORCEMENT_MAP_REPORT.md:14:- Dashboard, timeline, analytics, Carnos, documents/evidence, export/forget/destructive, connector, and Spotify links.
```

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_11_SAFETY_DATA_TARGET_TREND_BOUNDARY.md:197:Health/body data can be misleading when dashboards hide duplicate logs, treat missing values as zero, calculate averages from empty arrays, or display targets that were never set.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_11_SAFETY_DATA_TARGET_TREND_BOUNDARY.md:235:- `calories = null` means not logged.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_11_SAFETY_DATA_TARGET_TREND_BOUNDARY.md:236:- `calories = 0` means a recorded zero value.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_11_SAFETY_DATA_TARGET_TREND_BOUNDARY.md:238:- `sleep_hours = 0` means a recorded zero value, which should be treated carefully and not normalized away.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_16_5J_CARNOS_VISUAL_IDENTITY_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:9:This final chunk verifies that the Carnos visual/body/status layer is present across the intended surfaces while keeping all runtime systems deferred.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18B_SCHEMA_DISCOVERY_METRIC_SOURCE_MAP_REPORT.md:28:Phase 18B prevents later chunks from inventing fake metric sources or building dashboards on imaginary data. Later Phase 18 chunks must use the source map, acknowledge gaps, and ask for schema/migration details when needed.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18B_SCHEMA_DISCOVERY_METRIC_SOURCE_MAP_REPORT.md:3:Phase 18B adds static schema discovery and metric source mapping for Phase 18 Analytics/Experiments.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18C_METRIC_REGISTRY_DATA_QUALITY_REPORT.md:22:No fake metrics or fake analytics data are added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18C_METRIC_REGISTRY_DATA_QUALITY_REPORT.md:28:The metric registry prevents dashboards, trend engines, experiment evaluators, and Carnos explanations from inventing metric behavior. Every later analytics feature must respect source mode, data quality, cache eligibility, sensitivity, stale/cached/unsynced labels, and local runtime compatibility.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18C_METRIC_REGISTRY_DATA_QUALITY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 18C adds the canonical metric registry and data quality rules for Analytics/Experiments.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18O_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:11:Phase 18 added analytics and self-experiment foundations, metric quality, analytics snapshots, self-experiment contracts, insight quality/provenance, repository boundaries, deterministic trend/comparison/correlation analysis, experiment evaluation, analytics dashboard UI, self-experiment lab UI, Carnos analytics explanation boundaries, optional local Carnos runtime boundary, anti-demo-data/privacy audit, and final completion proof.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20X_MANUAL_WORKOUT_DEFERRED_CONNECTORS_REPORT.md:22:No schema was needed. This chunk does not add migrations, custom tracker tables, connector tables, wearable sync jobs, provider clients, imports, dashboard adapters, /privacy UI, Carnos tools, audit writes, or health data storage.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20X_MANUAL_WORKOUT_DEFERRED_CONNECTORS_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20X locks manual workout logging as the active health/body logging stance for Phase 20 and defines excluded or deferred connector boundaries so the app does not falsely imply Garmin, Echo, Alexa, wearable sync, automatic workout import, device APIs, or external health connectors exist before source, schema, provider, privacy, and review gates are satisfied.
```

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:1:# Phase 19G Privacy Levels and Carnos Access Permissions Report
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19G adds deterministic local privacy and Carnos permission boundaries for custom trackers. It blocks restricted tracker exposure, requires review for sensitive broad dashboard placement, keeps Carnos memory-candidate behavior behind review, and prevents silent Carnos reads, writes, and memory writes.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:15:- Privacy gates require Phase 19G Carnos permissions before review can proceed.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19K_EVIDENCE_ATTACHMENT_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19K adds deterministic local boundaries for custom tracker evidence attachments.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19K_EVIDENCE_ATTACHMENT_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:9:- Tracker and entry evidence links can represent documents, notes, web sources, timeline records, memory candidates, and external references.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19M_CUSTOM_TRACKER_DASHBOARD_UI_REPORT.md:3:Phase 19M adds the /custom-trackers dashboard route, local view model, and UI shell.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19M_CUSTOM_TRACKER_DASHBOARD_UI_REPORT.md:8:- The dashboard view model provides safe state messages, card contracts, template suggestions, primary actions, and boundary disclosures.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:10:Phase 19 added a safe custom tracker foundation with scope lock, core contracts, typed fields, entry validation, schema versioning, templates, privacy, Carnos proposal review queue, dashboard placement, timeline and analytics compatibility, evidence attachments, repository ownership boundaries, and the /custom-trackers dashboard UI.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2000:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19A_CUSTOM_TRACKERS_SCOPE_LOCK_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19A locks the expanded Phase 19 custom tracker scope before implementation. docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2010:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:10:Phase 19 added a safe custom tracker foundation with scope lock, core contracts, typed fields, entry validation, schema versioning, templates, privacy, Carnos proposal review queue, dashboard placement, timeline and analytics compatibility, evidence attachments, repository ownership boundaries, and the /custom-trackers dashboard UI.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2566:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18O_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:11:Phase 18 added analytics and self-experiment foundations, metric quality, analytics snapshots, self-experiment contracts, insight quality/provenance, repository boundaries, deterministic trend/comparison/correlation analysis, experiment evaluation, analytics dashboard UI, self-experiment lab UI, Carnos analytics explanation boundaries, optional local Carnos runtime boundary, anti-demo-data/privacy audit, and final completion proof.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2567:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18O_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:15:The final proof preserves Supabase/Postgres as online source of truth, keeps offline cache and sync execution as future boundaries, requires cached/stale/partial/missing/unsynced/deterministic disclosures, preserves offline current-info limitations, keeps local/offline context from being treated as online source of truth, keeps local runtime optional, preserves review-before-memory-write, and keeps action execution disabled.
```

```text
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19A_CUSTOM_TRACKERS_SCOPE_LOCK_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19A locks the expanded Phase 19 custom tracker scope before implementation. It records the official baseline, expanded feature list, 14-chunk implementation plan, feature-to-build-chunk map, safety boundaries, Carnos proposal/review rules, document/web/source compatibility, timeline and analytics compatibility, privacy/RLS/audit expectations, and final completion definition.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:10:- Custom tracker records cannot be marked as system trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:11:- Custom tracker stable keys are normalized.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:12:- Reserved system tracker keys are protected.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:15:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19B adds the core TypeScript domain contracts for custom trackers. It defines tracker records, field records, entry records, lifecycle statuses, privacy levels, stable key behavior, reserved system key protection, naming collision handling, ownership alignment, and the non-overridable system tracker boundary.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19B_CORE_TRACKER_DOMAIN_CONTRACTS_REPORT.md:9:- Core custom tracker contracts are available from src/lib/custom-trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19C_FIELD_TYPE_REGISTRY_VALIDATION_REPORT.md:14:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19C_FIELD_TYPE_REGISTRY_VALIDATION_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19C adds the field type registry and field validation rules for custom trackers. It covers allowed field kinds, field definition validation, required and optional value behavior, options JSON validation, select and multi-select checks, value boundaries for text, number, boolean, date, rating, duration, and JSON object fields, plus unit, normalization, privacy, and unknown-field reject/quarantine boundaries.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19C_FIELD_TYPE_REGISTRY_VALIDATION_REPORT.md:9:- Field type registry is available from src/lib/custom-trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19D_ENTRY_VALIDATION_VALUES_JSON_SAFETY_REPORT.md:15:- No fake tracker entries are allowed as runtime data.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19D_ENTRY_VALIDATION_VALUES_JSON_SAFETY_REPORT.md:17:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19D_ENTRY_VALIDATION_VALUES_JSON_SAFETY_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19D adds deterministic local entry validation for custom trackers. It validates entry ownership alignment, entry dates, notes, values_json shape, required fields, field value types, unknown fields, duplicate same-day entries, quarantine behavior, and no-fake-entry boundaries.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19D_ENTRY_VALIDATION_VALUES_JSON_SAFETY_REPORT.md:9:- Entry validation is available from src/lib/custom-trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19E_SCHEMA_VERSIONING_ARCHIVE_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:18:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19E_SCHEMA_VERSIONING_ARCHIVE_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19E adds deterministic local schema versioning and archive safety boundaries for custom trackers. It supports schema version parsing, formatting, comparison, incrementing, compatibility checks, deprecated-field read compatibility, field deprecation plans, tracker lifecycle transitions, field lifecycle transitions, tracker archive decisions, entry archive decisions, and blocked destructive removal decisions.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19E_SCHEMA_VERSIONING_ARCHIVE_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:9:- Schema versioning is available from src/lib/custom-trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:12:- Streaks are blocked for on-demand trackers.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:16:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19F adds deterministic local template and frequency semantics for custom trackers. It introduces a safe template library, template creation contracts requiring review before write, frequency rule validation, target count semantics, streak and missed-entry behavior, favorite/pinned/repeat-last preferences, and tracker readiness scoring for analytics, privacy, and dashboard placement.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:9:- Template library is available from src/lib/custom-trackers.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:17:- No runtime database call was added.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19G adds deterministic local privacy and Carnos permission boundaries for custom trackers. It blocks restricted tracker exposure, requires review for sensitive broad dashboard placement, keeps Carnos memory-candidate behavior behind review, and prevents silent Carnos reads, writes, and memory writes.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:9:- Tracker privacy policies are available from src/lib/custom-trackers.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19H adds deterministic local Carnos proposal contracts and a review-before-write queue boundary for custom trackers. Carnos can propose tracker creation, tracker improvement, field mapping, and message-to-entry drafts, but every proposal requires review before any write can occur.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:15:## CustomTrackers v1 stance
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:19:CustomTrackers is a safe preview and contract shell for v1.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:21:The current `/custom-trackers` surface intentionally has:
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:26:- no fake tracker entries;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:33:### Explicit post-v1 runtime scope
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:35:The following CustomTrackers capabilities are post-v1 unless a future source-of-truth update explicitly brings them forward:
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:37:- custom tracker table persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:38:- custom tracker field persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:39:- custom tracker entry persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:40:- dashboard placement persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:41:- Carnos-to-entry proposal persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:42:- custom tracker analytics from persisted entries;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:43:- custom tracker timeline/runtime integration;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:44:- custom tracker repository write flows;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:45:- custom tracker RLS-backed CRUD operations.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:49:Phase 21 must not treat CustomTrackers as a completed runtime tracker engine. It may polish the safe preview shell only if it preserves the no-runtime/no-write truth.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:65:- no Spotify account persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:74:### Explicit post-v1 runtime scope
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:76:The following Spotify capabilities are post-v1 unless a future source-of-truth update explicitly brings them forward:
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:82:- account/profile persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:88:- Spotify audit persistence;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_H_POST_V1_RUNTIME_BOUNDARY_LOCK.md:93:Phase 21 must not treat Spotify as connected. It may polish privacy/connector boundary UI only if it preserves the truth that Spotify runtime is post-v1.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:112:- no CustomTrackers runtime persistence claim;
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:123:Phase 21 must not silently implement or imply runtime systems that are explicitly post-v1 in this report.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:17:- CustomTrackers runtime persistence is post-v1.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:18:- Spotify OAuth/token/provider runtime is post-v1.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:3:Generated after the post-Phase-20 whole-project connectivity audit, runtime-read fixes, boundary wording cleanup, and post-v1 runtime boundary lock.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_I_PRE_PHASE_21_READINESS_AUDIT.md:45:| Spotify | no connector repository / token runtime | intentionally post-v1 runtime |

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2000:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19A_CUSTOM_TRACKERS_SCOPE_LOCK_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19A locks the expanded Phase 19 custom tracker scope before implementation. It records the official baseline, expanded feature list, 14-chunk implementation plan, feature-to-build-chunk map, safety boundaries, Carnos proposal/review rules, document/web/source compatibility, timeline and analytics compatibility, privacy/RLS/audit expectations, and final completion definition.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2001:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19F_TEMPLATES_FREQUENCY_SEMANTICS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19F adds deterministic local template and frequency semantics for custom trackers. It introduces a safe template library, template creation contracts requiring review before write, frequency rule validation, target count semantics, streak and missed-entry behavior, favorite/pinned/repeat-last preferences, and tracker readiness scoring for analytics, privacy, and dashboard placement.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2002:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:10:- Carnos tracker improvement proposals are represented.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2006:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19H adds deterministic local Carnos proposal contracts and a review-before-write queue boundary for custom trackers. Carnos can propose tracker creation, tracker improvement, field mapping, and message-to-entry drafts, but every proposal requires review before any write can occur.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2007:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19H_CARNOS_PROPOSALS_REVIEW_QUEUE_REPORT.md:9:- Carnos tracker creation proposals are represented.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:2010:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:10:Phase 19 added a safe custom tracker foundation with scope lock, core contracts, typed fields, entry validation, schema versioning, templates, privacy, Carnos proposal review queue, dashboard placement, timeline and analytics compatibility, evidence attachments, repository ownership boundaries, and the /custom-trackers dashboard UI.

docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6166:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_18A_B_COMPANION_PERSISTENCE_OFFLINE_AI_REPORT.md:9:- Carnos persistence proof report
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6198:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19A_CUSTOM_TRACKERS_SCOPE_LOCK_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19A locks the expanded Phase 19 custom tracker scope before implementation. It records the official baseline, expanded feature list, 14-chunk implementation plan, feature-to-build-chunk map, safety boundaries, Carnos proposal/review rules, document/web/source compatibility, timeline and analytics compatibility, privacy/RLS/audit expectations, and final completion definition.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6203:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19G_PRIVACY_CARNOS_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19G adds deterministic local privacy and Carnos permission boundaries for custom trackers. It blocks restricted tracker exposure, requires review for sensitive broad dashboard placement, keeps Carnos memory-candidate behavior behind review, and prevents silent Carnos reads, writes, and memory writes.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6207:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19K_EVIDENCE_ATTACHMENT_BOUNDARIES_REPORT.md:5:Phase 19K adds deterministic local boundaries for custom tracker evidence attachments.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6209:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19M_CUSTOM_TRACKER_DASHBOARD_UI_REPORT.md:3:Phase 19M adds the /custom-trackers dashboard route, local view model, and UI shell.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6211:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_19N_FINAL_COMPLETION_REPORT.md:10:Phase 19 added a safe custom tracker foundation with scope lock, core contracts, typed fields, entry validation, schema versioning, templates, privacy, Carnos proposal review queue, dashboard placement, timeline and analytics compatibility, evidence attachments, repository ownership boundaries, and the /custom-trackers dashboard UI.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6241:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20G_SENSITIVE_LOCKS_DOMAIN_PERMISSIONS_REPORT.md:21:No schema was needed. This chunk does not add migrations, sensitive lock persistence, domain setting rows, RLS policies, repositories, or live permission writes.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6244:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20H_CARNOS_ACCESS_MATRIX_REPORT.md:21:No schema was needed. This chunk does not add migrations, Carnos permission persistence, RLS policies, repositories, connector runtime authorization, or Spotify runtime authorization.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6250:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20K_DATA_SCOPE_SELECTOR_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20K defines the shared data scope selector for export, forget, hide, archive, destructive action previews, privacy review queue items, connector data previews, Spotify data previews, memory controls, custom trackers, documents, current-info sources, analytics, timeline, and audit surfaces.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6261:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20N_AUDIT_VIEWER_APPEND_ONLY_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20N defines Audit Viewer model, filters, detail panels, actor/source/target metadata, linked manifests, linked review requests, linked source/evidence references, connector references, Spotify references, redaction behavior, append-only audit boundary, Carnos audit restrictions, protected audit events, export relationship, blocked reasons, badges, and verification rules before live audit viewer UI or audit persistence changes exist.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6265:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20P_DATA_RETENTION_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:17:- Memory candidate, saved memory, export, private session, review request, audit preview, connector, Spotify, source/evidence, and custom tracker privacy proposal retention.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6266:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20P_DATA_RETENTION_BOUNDARY_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20P defines retention policy taxonomy, expiration, archive, review-after, forget-candidate, export expiry, private session expiry, review request expiry, connector action proposal expiry, Spotify action proposal expiry, source/evidence retention, custom tracker privacy proposal retention, and privacy-safe retention behavior before jobs, schema, or runtime enforcement exist.
docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20Z_J_FULL_PROJECT_PHASE_0_20_TRUTH_AUDIT.md:6281:docs/phase-reports/PHASE_20S_SPOTIFY_CONNECTOR_FOUNDATION_REPORT.md:3:Phase 20S defines Spotify connector identity, developer setup boundary, redirect URI boundary, OAuth PKCE stance, environment variable boundary, scope groups, connection status model, provider policy boundary, token boundary, account profile boundary, privacy mode behavior, emergency lockdown behavior, Carnos access stance, audit events, blocked reasons, and badges before any OAuth route, callback route, token storage, provider call, UI card, or persistence exists.
```

```text
4533:  retrieval_mode: "preview_only" | "approved_memory_only" | "knowledge_only" | "disabled";
4538:export interface MemoryUsageLogRow {
4541:  memory_item_id: string | null;
4542:  knowledge_item_id: string | null;
4546:  used_in_carnos_response: boolean;
4552:export interface MemoryReviewQueueRow {
4555:  memory_candidate_id: string | null;
4556:  memory_item_id: string | null;
4565:export interface MemoryRetrievalEventRow {
4574:    | "approved_memory_only"
4575:    | "knowledge_only"
4585:  used_by_carnos: boolean;
4586:  retrieved_memory_ids: string[];
4587:  retrieved_knowledge_ids: string[];
4588:  blocked_memory_ids: string[];
```
